question.text: Which of the following is a mandatory prerequisite for claiming benefits under a Double Tax Avoidance Agreement (DTAA)?

answer.text: A certificate from the assessing officer of India
A No Objection Certificate (NOC) from SEBI
A Permanent Account Number (PAN) issued by the foreign country
A Tax Residency Certificate (TRC) obtained from the government of the country of residence

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Tax Residency Certificate (TRC) is mandatory for claiming DTAA benefits. It must be obtained from the government of the country where the taxpayer claims to be a resident. Without a valid TRC, DTAA benefits cannot be claimed. **The Trap:** PAN is an Indian concept; foreign countries do not issue PAN. SEBI has no role in DTAA matters. The assessing officer may verify the claim but does not issue the prerequisite certificate. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) exams test TDS provisions for NRI investors where TRC plays a role in claiming lower withholding rates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind NRI clients to obtain their TRC before the financial year begins — without it, the payer will deduct tax at the full domestic rate, and claiming a refund later is cumbersome.
